extracted_information: For AI/IDE environments, particularly codegen tools that execute untrusted code, secure runtime environments are critical. Traditional containers are insufficient due to shared host kernels and risks like privilege escalation, container escape, cross-tenant access, and denial-of-service. MicroVMs provide a superior sandboxing approach, offering VM-grade security isolation with container-like performance. Technologies like Firecracker (developed by AWS) and Kata Containers enable fast-starting, lightweight virtual machines with dedicated kernels, separated namespaces, and strict resource quotas, making them ideal for securely executing untrusted code from LLMs or user prompts. Northflank is presented as a platform leveraging Kata Containers and Firecracker to provide scalable, secure microVM-backed execution environments for multi-tenant AI workloads.

microvm_isolation_properties: runs in a sandboxed environment with its own kernel
no access to host processes or containers

items: Isolation of each user in a sandbox environment with resource and network boundaries
Combine container-like performance with VM-grade security isolation
Perfect for executing untrusted code from user's LLM prompt
Spin up microVM-backed containers in seconds
Uses Kata Containers for Firecracker-grade security
Multi-tenant isolation (fully separated namespace by user, tenant, team, or use case)
Deploys any container image into a secure microVM with full isolation (own kernel, vNIC)
Support for persistent or ephemeral execution
